questions.question: Who wrote the story ‘The Necklace’?
questions.answer: The story ‘The Necklace’ was written by Guy de Maupassant.

questions.question: Who is the main character in the story?
questions.answer: Mme Loisel is the main character in the story.

questions.question: What kind of life does Mme Loisel desire?
questions.answer: Mme Loisel desires a luxurious and wealthy life.

questions.question: What is Mr Loisel’s occupation?
questions.answer: Mr Loisel works as a clerk.

questions.question: Why is Mme Loisel unhappy?
questions.answer: She is unhappy because she is dissatisfied with her life.

questions.question: What invitation do the Loisels receive?
questions.answer: They receive an invitation to a minister’s ball.

questions.question: Why does Mme Loisel feel upset about the invitation?
questions.answer: She feels upset because she has no suitable dress.

questions.question: How much money does Mr Loisel give for the dress?
questions.answer: He gives 400 francs for the dress.

questions.question: From whom does Mme Loisel borrow the necklace?
questions.answer: She borrows the necklace from Mme Forestier.

questions.question: How does Mme Loisel feel at the ball?
questions.answer: She feels happy, proud, and admired.

questions.question: What happens to the necklace after the ball?
questions.answer: The necklace gets lost.

questions.question: What do the Loisels decide to do after losing the necklace?
questions.answer: They decide to replace the necklace.

questions.question: How do the Loisels get money to replace the necklace?
questions.answer: They borrow money.

questions.question: For how many years do the Loisels suffer?
questions.answer: They suffer for ten years.

questions.question: What kind of life does Mme Loisel live after the loss?
questions.answer: She lives a life of poverty and hard work.

questions.question: Whom does Mme Loisel meet after ten years?
questions.answer: She meets Mme Forestier.

questions.question: What truth is revealed at the end of the story?
questions.answer: The necklace was fake.

questions.question: What literary device is used strongly in the ending?
questions.answer: Irony is strongly used in the ending.

questions.question: What is the main theme of the story?
questions.answer: The main theme is appearance versus reality.

questions.question: Which quality of Mr Loisel is highlighted?
questions.answer: His patience and sacrifice are highlighted.

questions.question: Why does Mme Loisel not tell the truth immediately?
questions.answer: She is afraid of humiliation.

questions.question: What lesson does the story teach?
questions.answer: The story teaches honesty and contentment.

questions.question: What type of story is ‘The Necklace’?
questions.answer: It is a realistic short story.

questions.question: Which character represents wealth?
questions.answer: Mme Forestier represents wealth.

questions.question: What causes the suffering of the Loisels?
questions.answer: Pride and dishonesty cause their suffering.
